name: Salted Pumpkin Seeds
recipeInstructions:
1. Use seeds from the Halloween pumpkin.
2. Combine water and salt in a saucepan. Cook over medium heat, stirring until the salt fully dissolves. Allow the mixture to cool completely.
3. Add the pumpkin seeds to the cooled saltwater solution and soak them overnight. After soaking, drain the seeds thoroughly and pat them dry with paper towels.
4. Spread the drained seeds on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Place in an oven preheated to 300°F (150°C). Bake for about 1 hour or until the seeds are completely dry.
5. Once dried, store the salted pumpkin seeds in an airtight container. They yield approximately 2 cups and provide 8 servings of 1/4 cup each, totaling 92 calories per serving with 2 fat exchanges.
Source: Diabetic High Fiber Cookbook by Mary Jane Finsand (c. 1985) - Shared and not tested by Elizabeth Rodier in October 1993.
